description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a blooming lily flower with a prominent white corolla and yellow accents radiating from the center. The flower features six elongated petals, some slightly overlapping, with visible orange stamens and a central pistil. Surrounding the flower are several green, lanceolate leaves attached to a slender, upright stem. The leaves are arranged alternately along the stem, with some showing a slightly glossy texture. The overall structure is delicate, with the flower positioned at the terminal end of the stem, making it the focal point. The coloration and organic form suggest a fresh, natural appearance suitable for parks, gardens, or urban green spaces.
description: A white lily flower with yellow accents, orange stamens, and green lanceolate leaves on a slender stem. The flower is prominently displayed at the top of the plant.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open face of the lily, with all six petals, orange stamens, and central pistil clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the flower, the curvature of the petals, and the arrangement of leaves along the stem.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the petals and the full length of the stem with leaves extending outward.
description_view_3: Side/rear view focusing on the stem and the alternate arrangement of the lanceolate leaves, with the flower partially visible from behind.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration with slight overlapping at the base, displaying actinomorphic (radial) symmetry typical of lilies.
